project.title: Hybrid Entity Labelling for Domain-Specific Text
project.context: Efficient data labelling and entity extraction for domain-specific applications (e.g., chemical engineering, medicine, finance) using a combination of AutoML, gazetteers, and custom NER models.
project.core_question: For domain-specific data, should I use AutoML for labelling, combine AutoML + NER gazetteers, or train a custom NER model? What is the most efficient and modern approach?

overview.goal: Transform unstructured domain text into structured, labelled data efficiently and accurately.
overview.challenge: Manual labelling is time-consuming and domain-specific jargon limits off-the-shelf models' accuracy.

approaches_comparison.description: Use AutoML tools to auto-label text into pre-trained categories like sentiment or topic.
pros: Good for general-purpose or prototype-level insights
Easily integrates into dashboards or pipelines
cons: Less suitable for highly technical domains

approaches_comparison.description: Use dictionaries, regex, or rule-based systems to tag known entities from domain vocabularies.
pros: Quick to implement for stable vocabularies
approaches_comparison.best_use_case: Domains with stable vocabularies like product codes, drug lists, or company names.

approaches_comparison.description: Train or fine-tune a spaCy or Hugging Face model to recognize domain-specific entities.
pros: Scalable and adaptable to evolving data
approaches_comparison.best_use_case: Complex, evolving, context-heavy domains like chemistry, law, or finance.

modern_hybrid_pipeline.description: Modern workflows combine GenAI-assisted labelling, gazetteer augmentation, and custom fine-tuning to balance speed, accuracy, and domain adaptation.

steps.process: Use a large language model (e.g., GPT-4, Claude, Llama) to extract entities or relationships from text using structured prompts.
steps.example_prompt: Extract all chemical substances and their quantities mentioned in this text.
steps.output: Generate pseudo-labelled data (e.g., JSON or CSV) for review and cleanup.

steps.process: Integrate domain-specific vocabularies or gazetteers (e.g., PubChem, internal safety DBs).
steps.goal: Enhance label consistency and recall by combining curated and auto-labelled entities.

steps.process: Fine-tune a spaCy, Hugging Face, or BERT-based NER model using cleaned hybrid-labelled data.
steps.evaluation: Assess F1, precision, and recall; retrain periodically as new entities emerge.

modern_hybrid_pipeline.summary: Modern teams bootstrap labelling with GenAI, refine with gazetteers, and stabilize with custom fine-tuned NER models.

domain_example.example_task: Extract chemical compounds and their quantities from safety or incident reports.
workflow: Use GPT to extract entities and quantities → save as pseudo-labelled JSON
Augment with PubChem chemical list gazetteer for lookup tagging
Fine-tune a spaCy transformer model on the merged dataset
Evaluate and retrain as new compounds appear
domain_example.outcome: A living, evolving NER system that improves accuracy without requiring full manual annotation each time.

summary.modern_best_practice: LLM-assisted labelling + Gazetteer augmentation + Custom fine-tuning
